description: A vertical public-service-announcement graphic from ICE promoting its VOICE (Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement) office. The right portion is a soft, warm-lit photograph of two pairs of hands clasped together in a comforting, supportive gesture, with a person in a light-blue top seated and slightly blurred in the background, suggesting consolation or counseling. The left third is a semi-transparent blue panel overlay carrying white sans-serif text: 'If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to' followed by a large bold 'HELP.' An orange diagonal accent shape appears at the lower right of the photo. A white bottom bar contains contact info: '1-855-48-VOICE' and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE' (with 'VOICE' emphasized in blue/orange), a vertical divider, then the VOICE logo - a blue stylized 'V' checkmark beside 'VOICE' with the subtitle 'VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT.' The style is a polished, empathetic corporate-PSA layout in blue and orange brand colors. The messaging promotes a victim-services hotline, but it also reinforces the political framing (central to the VOICE program's creation) that crimes are committed by immigrants. Target audience is purported crime victims and the broader public.
notable_text: If you're the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we're here to HELP - 1-855-48-VOICE / ICE.GOV/VOICE - VOICE: Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement
